design_language.aesthetic: Bold, high-contrast, and aggressive. The design moves away from 'appetite appeal' in some ads to focus purely on 'value appeal' and 'transparency'.
design_language.visual_approach: Heavy use of split-screens to create a binary choice (Good vs. Bad). Uses UI mockups and receipt breakdowns as the primary visual hooks.

color_logic.primary_brand_color: Deep Forest Green (#004d00 approx) - Used as the base for the 'Toing' side.
color_logic.accent_color: Neon Pink / Magenta (#ff00cc approx) - Used for buttons, logos, and key highlights.
color_logic.competitor_color: Red / Coral (#ff4d4d approx) - Used exclusively for the 'Other Apps' or 'Competitor' side to signal danger/expensive.
color_logic.secondary_accent: Lime Green - Used for 'Free' tags and positive indicators.

typography.headline_font: Bold, condensed sans-serif (Impact style). All caps for maximum authority.
typography.body_font: Clean sans-serif (likely Inter or Roboto) for UI text readability.

design_language.overall_aesthetic: Versus battle style. Divides the screen vertically into two distinct worlds.
design_language.visual_approach: Left side (Green) represents the brand; Right side (Red) represents the competitor.

color_logic.background_split: 50/50 vertical split. Left is Brand Green (Checkerboard pattern); Right is Competitor Red (Solid).
color_logic.ui_elements: White cards on both sides for neutrality, but price tags are Green (Left) vs Red (Right).

typography.headline_style: Bold, White, Sans-Serif. Centered in respective color blocks.
typography.hierarchy: Headline -> Product Image -> Price -> Phone Mockup.
typography.text_placement: Top third for headlines; Middle for product cards; Bottom for phone UI.

graphics_and_images.hero_graphics: Two hands holding smartphones displaying the app interface.
graphics_and_images.product_images: Small thumbnails of 'Chicken Popcorn' inside white speech bubbles/cards.
graphics_and_images.badges: A pink 'Lowest Price Guaranteed' stamp overlapping the split line.

message_delivery.strategy: Visual proof. Shows the exact same item at a lower price on their app vs. a competitor.
message_delivery.emotional_trigger: Fear of missing out (FOMO) on savings; feeling smart for switching.

design_language.overall_aesthetic: Informational graphic design. Looks like a screenshot of a bill.
design_language.visual_approach: Side-by-side comparison of two 'receipts' or checkout screens.

color_logic.background: Solid Deep Green with subtle checkerboard texture.
color_logic.highlighting: Green text for 'FREE' items on the left; Red text for prices on the right.
color_logic.buttons: Pink 'To Pay' button (Left) vs Red 'To Pay' button (Right).
color_logic.color_usage: Uses color to guide the eye to the 'Total' amount at the bottom.

typography.headline_style: Large, conversational white text at the top ('Those extra fees?...').
typography.ui_text: Small, legible sans-serif mimicking a real app interface.
typography.text_placement: Headline top center; Receipts side-by-side in the lower two-thirds.

graphics_and_images.hero_graphics: Two large white cards mimicking mobile app checkout screens.
graphics_and_images.product_image: Small thumbnail of a 'McVeggie Burger' at the top of each card.
graphics_and_images.badges: Pink 'Lowest Price Guaranteed' stamp at the bottom left.

message_delivery.strategy: Itemized comparison. Shows exactly *where* the competitor charges extra (delivery, packaging, platform fee).
message_delivery.emotional_trigger: Frustration with hidden fees; relief at transparency.

color_logic.highlighting: Green 'FREE' text vs Red price text.
color_logic.color_usage: Consistent coding: Pink/Green = Good; Red = Bad.

typography.headline_style: Large white text ('Hidden charges? Not our style!').

message_delivery.strategy: Reinforces the 'No Hidden Charges' narrative with a different product example.

color_logic.highlighting: Green 'FREE' text vs Red price text.

typography.headline_style: Large white text ('Hidden charges? Not our style!').

design_language.overall_aesthetic: Appetite-focused but with urgent sale graphics.
design_language.visual_approach: Large hero food image dominates the bottom; bold typography at the top.

color_logic.background: Deep Green with Checkerboard pattern (top left).
color_logic.text_colors: White for main text; Pink for the price ('₹29').
color_logic.accents: Lime Green pill shape for the time constraint ('6-9 PM').
color_logic.color_usage: Pink is used to highlight the 'Deal' elements (Price, Free Delivery tag).

typography.headline_style: Bold, condensed sans-serif. 'PIZZA AT' is white; Price is Pink.
typography.branding: 'BROKEHOURS' logo at the top right in a glitch/distorted font style.
typography.text_placement: Top third for offer details; Bottom two-thirds for food image.

graphics_and_images.hero_image: Large, high-saturation photo of a pizza with a cheese pull slice being lifted.
graphics_and_images.graphic_elements: Pink arrow/tag pointing to the food saying 'FREE DELIVERY'.

message_delivery.strategy: Scarcity + Extreme Value. The price (₹29) is the hero.

design_language.overall_aesthetic: Similar to Ad 5 but vertical orientation.
design_language.visual_approach: Text on left, Food on right. Dynamic diagonal composition.

color_logic.background: Deep Green with Checkerboard pattern (right side).
color_logic.text_colors: White and Pink mix. 'BEST ROLLS' is white; 'STARTING ₹29' has Pink price.
color_logic.accents: Pink box for the 'Flash sale' details.
color_logic.color_usage: Pink highlights the 'Call to Action' elements.

typography.headline_style: Bold, stacked text on the left. 'GET THE BEST ROLLS'.
typography.text_placement: Left aligned text block; Right aligned food image.

graphics_and_images.hero_image: Large, vertical close-up of a roll/wrap. Shows texture and filling clearly.
graphics_and_images.graphic_elements: Pink tag 'FREE DELIVERY' overlaid on the food.
graphics_and_images.info_box: Pink square box on the left containing the time details.

message_delivery.strategy: Vertical format optimized for mobile stories. Focuses on the 'Starting at' price point.
